_comment: Example camera timing config for Basler daA3840-45um (Pylon dart). NOT real measured data — placeholder values for reference. Generate real configs using the engineering plugin camera characterization tool.

skip_frames._comment: Number of frames to grab and discard after each state change before the camera output reflects the new state. These override FrameValidity.SKIP_FRAMES defaults (2 for all sources). Measured via LED/gain/exposure settle tests in camera characterization.

frame_intervals_ms._comment: Mean frame interval at various exposure times (ms). Used for estimating capture duration and protocol time estimates.

_comment_af_drain: Whether Z moves during autofocus produce stale frames that must be drained. false means focus score is reliable immediately after Z move (global shutter behavior).
_comment_focus_noise: Coefficient of variation (%) of focus score when nothing changes. Lower is better. Values above 5% indicate noisy sensor or unstable illumination.
_comment_min_exposure: Minimum exposure time (ms) that produces a usable focus score. Below this, SNR is too low for reliable autofocus.
_comment_hysteresis: Whether the Z stage shows different focus scores approaching from above vs below. If true, autofocus should always approach from the same direction.
_comment_roi: Smallest center ROI fraction (of full frame) that correlates >0.95 with quarter-frame focus score. Smaller ROI = faster AF computation.
_comment_binning: Whether 2x2 binning produces reliable focus scores (CV within 2x of 1x1). If true, AF can use binned frames for faster throughput.

led_warmup_drift_pct._comment: LED intensity drift (%) over first 60 seconds after turn-on. Values above 2% suggest waiting before quantitative imaging.

dark_noise_stddev._comment: Standard deviation of dark frame pixel values (no illumination). Higher values indicate more sensor noise. Useful for setting minimum signal thresholds.

_comment_crosstalk: Whether illuminating one LED channel produces measurable signal change on adjacent channels. If true, sequential rather than simultaneous multi-channel capture is recommended.
